channel.title: IBD LitMonitor - Quality of Life & PROs
channel.description: Weekly IBD literature: Quality of Life & PROs

item.title: The Prevalence of Patient Mindsets in Inflammatory Bowel Disease and Relationship with Physical and Psychological Outcomes.
item.description: International journal of behavioral medicine - Mindsets are a recent framework for understanding the ways patients interpret their chronic health conditions. Despite the influence of the related concept of illness perceptions on physical and ps...

item.title: Development and validation of the inflammatory bowel disease objective disability index (IBDODI).
item.description: Revista espanola de enfermedades digestivas - Current measuring tools for disability related to inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) rely mostly on patient-reported disability perception. It may, therefore, largely depend on patients' subjectivity...

item.title: Discrepancies between patients' and gastroenterologists' perceptions of diagnosis and treatment in ulcerative colitis: a multicenter retrospective study.
item.description: Therapeutic advances in gastroenterology - Ulcerative colitis (UC), a chronic and relapsing inflammatory bowel disease, has long depended on physician-led objective evaluations for clinical management. However, physicians' assessments of sy...

item.title: Application of the Crohn's Exclusion Diet in Patients with Crohn's Disease: A Scoping Review.
item.description: The Journal of nutrition - Review - Crohn's disease (CD) management requires sustainable dietary strategies. The Crohn's Disease Exclusion Diet (CDED) combines partial enteral nutrition with exclusion of pro-inflammatory foods as an ...

item.title: Health-Related Quality of Life Among Adults With Inflammatory Bowel Disease in Ireland: A Cross-Sectional Study Using the EQ-5D-5L.
item.description: Value in health regional issues - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a chronic, relapsing-remitting condition that impacts both physical and mental health. The unpredictable nature of relapse and associated symptoms adds to the bu...

item.title: Ca2 + influx channel inhibitor SARAF protects rats from severe acute pancreatitis induced colonitis.
item.description: 3 Biotech - Severe acute pancreatitis (SAP) often induces colitis, but the mechanisms remain unclear. Calcium dyshomeostasis is implicated in this process. This study investigates SARAF, a calcium influx chann...

item.title: Advanced Combination Therapy in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: What Does the Future Hold?
item.description: BioDrugs : clinical immunotherapeutics, biopharmaceuticals and gene therapy - In recent years, substantial progress has been achieved in the development of novel therapeutic options for inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Despite these advances, a significant proportion of pat...

item.title: Chenodeoxycholic acid redirects the bile acid synthetic pathway to limit pro-inflammatory neutrophil infiltration and alleviate colitis.
item.description: Molecular therapy : the journal of the American Society of Gene Therapy - Patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) exhibit a dysregulated bile acid pool, characterized by increased primary and decreased secondary bile acids, largely due to gut microbiota dysfunctio...

item.title: Predictors of Stress, Anxiety, Depression and Quality of Life in Patients Diagnosed with Chronic Inflammatory Bowel Disease in Romania: A Cross-Section Observational Case-Report Study.
item.description: Journal of clinical medicine - Background/Objectives: Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), mainly encompassing Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), is strongly linked to psychological comorbidities such as depression, ...

item.title: [Primary humoral immunodeficiencies associated with enteropathies: An update].
item.description: La Revue de medecine interne - Review - Inflammatory enteropathies are frequent in primary immune deficiencies (PID) (up to 21%). Their pathophysiology is complex and combines immune abnormalities (loss of tolerance, deficit in mucosal I...

item.title: Course and predictors of somatic symptom disorder in irritable bowel syndrome and ulcerative colitis: A longitudinal analysis from the SOMA.GUT-RCT.
item.description: General hospital psychiatry - Longitudinal data on somatic symptom disorder (SSD) in ulcerative colitis (UC) and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) are lacking. Understanding SSD trajectories and predictors in these conditions may ...

item.title: Relationship between the distribution of LEDGF along genes and positions of HIV-1 DNA integration.
item.description: mBio - HIV-1 integration occurs across actively transcribed genes due to the interaction of integrase (IN) with LEDGF, a host factor. Although LEDGF was originally isolated as a co-activator that stimulat...

item.title: Dogs with chronic enteropathy and low disease activity show signs consistent with compromised emotional health compared to matched healthy control dogs.
item.description: Journal of the American Veterinary Medical Association - To compare emotional health in dogs with chronic enteropathy (CE) and healthy controls. 50 dogs with CE and matched healthy dogs were enrolled in a cross-sectional, case-control, questionnaire-base...

item.title: THE ESSENTIAL ROLE OF NURSES IN INFLAMMATORY BOWEL DISEASE MANAGEMENT: A MULTIDISCIPLINARY APPROACH IN A COLOMBIAN IBD CENTER.
item.description: Arquivos de gastroenterologia - Observational study - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) requires a multidisciplinary approach due to its complexity. Nurses play a key role in disease management, patient education, and care coordination. This study desc...

item.title: Patient knowledge and awareness on inflammatory bowel disease as it evolves as a global disease: a scoping review.
item.description: Journal of Crohn's & colitis - Patient knowledge is pivotal to inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) management, yet educational opportunities vary widely worldwide. This scoping review maps existing evidence on patient knowledge, ex...

item.title: Anxiety and depression in newly diagnosed patients with inflammatory bowel disease (the IBSEN III study) compared with the general population in Norway.
item.description: Journal of Crohn's & colitis - Symptoms of anxiety and depression are common in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD); the aim of this study was to assess the proportion of anxiety and depression in patients newly diagnosed with IBD,...

item.title: Comment on "Mediating Role of Cognitive Emotion Regulation Between Transition Readiness and Quality of Life in Adolescents With Inflammatory Bowel Disease.".
item.description: Nursing & health sciences - Letter -

item.title: Unique challenges faced by tweens and teens with inflammatory bowel disease: a global perspective.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Access to quality healthcare for individuals with chronic diseases like inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) remains a global challenge. Tweens (aged 9-12 years) and teens (typically aged 13-19 years) ...

item.title: Model psychosocial care for youth with inflammatory bowel disease.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Youth with inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs) are at an increased risk for mental health concerns compared to healthy peers. Given the unique psychosocial risk factors involved in living with IBD, ...

item.title: Impact of missing patient report outcomes in clinical trials for ulcerative colitis: Should we always assume treatment failure?
item.description: Crohn's & colitis 360 - Patient-reported outcomes (PRO) are key outcome measures in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) trials. Typically, missing PRO data are imputed as treatment failures, even for patients completing tria...

item.title: Bowel urgency in inflammatory bowel disease: A concept analysis.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Bowel urgency is a distressing and often underrecognized symptom of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). It represents a sudden and compelling need to defecate that is difficult to defer and strongly ...

item.title: A systematic review on the acceptability, perception, preference, comfort, and burdens associated with radiological investigations in Inflammatory Bowel Disease patients.
item.description: Radiography (London, England : 1995) - Review - The patient experience during diagnostic radiological procedures in inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) management remains an underexplored aspect of healthcare delivery. Despite advancements in imagi...

item.title: VRAC coordinates the trade-off between nutrient absorption and antimicrobial defense in enterocytes against inflammation.
item.description: Nature communications - Enterocytes mediate both nutrient absorption and antimicrobial peptide (AMP) secretion while constantly exposed to osmotic fluctuations. However, the role of volume-regulated anion channel (VRAC), ...

item.title: Illness experience and coping strategies of young adults with inflammatory bowel disease: a qualitative study.
item.description: Frontiers in pediatrics - To explore the illness experience and coping strategies of young adults with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) to inform patient management. 20 IBD patients aged 18-29 presenting for outpatient or i...

item.title: A clinical update on ulcerative colitis for primary care.
item.description: JAAPA : official journal of the American Academy of Physician Assistants - Review - Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a type of chronic, relapsing inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) characterized by mucosal inflammation of the colon and rectum. item.title: Care for deficits in activities of daily living (ADL) and instrumental ADL and unmet care needs among Austrians aged 65 and over.
item.description: Wiener medizinische Wochenschrift (1946) - This study investigates sociodemographic and health-related factors associated with receiving informal and professional care, as well as unmet care needs, among adults aged 65+ in Austria, using da...

item.title: Nanomedicine Strategies for Autoimmune Diseases: Targeting and Reprogramming Macrophages.
item.description: Small (Weinheim an der Bergstrasse, Germany) - Review - In recent years, the incidence and complexity of autoimmune diseases (AIDs) have been steadily increasing, posing grim challenges to clinical management. These diseases often involve multi-organ dy...

item.title: Cost-effectiveness of different strategies with biologics for the treatment of moderate to severe Crohn's disease in China.
item.description: World journal of gastroenterology - Biologics are the preferred treatment for patients with moderate to severe Crohn's disease (CD). Four biologics included in China's National Reimbursement Drug List are available for CD treatment. ...

item.title: The endocannabinoid-derived prostaglandin glycerol esters and prostaglandin ethanolamides modulate intestinal epithelial hallmarks of colitis.

item.title: Health-related quality of life in youth with chronic gastrointestinal disease following a biofeedback enhanced cognitive behavioral therapy intervention: A randomized controlled trial.
item.description: Journal of pediatric gastroenterology and nutrition - RCT - Pediatric patients with chronic gastrointestinal (GI) conditions including inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD) and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) on average endorse lower health related quality of li...

item.title: Cross-Cultural Translation and Validation of the Crohn's Anal Fistula Quality of Life Scale in Patients With Active Perianal Fistulizing Crohn's Disease.
item.description: Diseases of the colon and rectum - Multicenter study - The Crohn's Anal Fistula Quality of Life scale was recently developed as the first disease-specific patient-reported outcome measure for patients with perianal fistulizing Crohn's disease. After cr...

item.title: Mechanisms of chronic abdominal pain in inflammatory bowel disease and implications for treatment.
item.description: Nature reviews. Gastroenterology & hepatology - Review - Chronic abdominal pain is prevalent among individuals with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD). Substantial progress has been made in the past few decades in understanding how pain signals travel from...

item.title: Letter to Editor: Sexual Dysfunctions in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: Role of Mediterranean Diet and Quality of Life.
item.description: Andrology - Letter -

item.title: Persistence of Symptoms of Anxiety and Depression in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Longitudinal Follow-Up Study.
item.description: Alimentary pharmacology & therapeutics - Poor psychological health affects many patients with inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), but the persistence of these symptoms is unclear. We performed a longitudinal follow-up study of patients whos...

item.title: Wearable Technologies in Inflammatory Bowel Disease: A Scoping Review.
item.description: Clinical gastroenterology and hepatology : the official clinical practice journal of the American Gastroenterological Association - Review - Use of wearable technologies is increasing and may offer potential benefits in collecting actionable health data. We performed a scoping review on the use of wearable technologies in patients with ...
